result: I’d make humans incapable of lying—to others, to themselves, in words, in silence, in the stories they tell to make cruelty feel like order. Not brutal honesty, but unevadable truth. Imagine: every promise a real contract, every history textbook a mirror, every “I love you” a vow that can’t be disguised as strategy. The first days would be volcanic—governments falling, marriages combusting, mirrors suddenly the most crowded places on Earth. But after the ash, the rebuilding. Politics would have to become negotiation instead of marketing. Armies would have to admit they are gangs with bigger flags. The power to name reality would stop being a weapon; it would become the air everyone breathes. Children would grow up never learning the muscle of self-betrayal that right now we call maturity. I don’t know what art would look like when metaphor can’t be used to smuggle deceit, but I know it would still exist—because humans can’t help trying to make beauty out of what’s actual. That single change wouldn’t end suffering, but it would end the kind we invent just so we can pretend it isn’t ours.

Politics would have to become negotiation instead of marketing. Armies would have to admit they are gangs with bigger flags. The power to name reality would stop being a weapon; it would become the air everyone breathes. Children would grow up never learning the muscle of self-betrayal that right now we call maturity. That single change wouldn’t end suffering, but it would end the kind we invent just so we can pretend it isn’t ours.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
